title: Create 'databases' and 'sigma1' Kubernetes Namespaces
description: Create the 'databases' namespace for infrastructure components and the 'sigma1' namespace for application services.
details: Use `kubectl create namespace databases` and `kubectl create namespace sigma1` to provision the required Kubernetes namespaces.
test_strategy: Verify both 'databases' and 'sigma1' namespaces exist using `kubectl get ns databases sigma1`.

title: Provision 'sigma1-assets' S3/R2 Bucket and Generate Credentials
description: Provision an S3/R2 compatible bucket named 'sigma1-assets' for storing application assets and generate necessary access credentials.
details: Use Cloudflare Terraform or equivalent to create the 'sigma1-assets' bucket in the designated R2 account. Subsequently, generate an API token with read/write permissions specifically for this bucket. Securely store the generated access key ID and secret access key. Assume Terraform state management is handled externally.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1-assets' bucket exists and is accessible using `rclone lsjson r2:sigma1-assets` or by checking the Cloudflare R2 dashboard. Use the generated credentials with `rclone` or `aws s3` CLI to perform a test upload and download to the 'sigma1-assets' bucket, verifying read/write access.

title: Deploy CloudNative-PG Operator
description: Deploy the CloudNative-PG operator into the 'databases' namespace to enable declarative management of PostgreSQL instances.
details: Apply the CloudNative-PG operator YAML manifests or use its Helm chart to deploy it into the 'databases' namespace. Ensure the operator pods are running and healthy.
test_strategy: Verify the CloudNative-PG operator pod is running in the 'databases' namespace using `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloudnative-pg`.

title: Deploy Redis Operator
description: Deploy a Redis operator into the 'databases' namespace to manage Redis/Valkey instances.
details: Apply the chosen Redis operator's YAML manifests or use its Helm chart to deploy it into the 'databases' namespace. Ensure the operator pods are running and healthy. The specific operator (e.g., Spotahome, OT-Container-Kit) is assumed to be pre-selected.
test_strategy: Verify the Redis operator pod is running in the 'databases' namespace using `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app=redis-operator` (adjust label as per actual operator deployment).

title: Set up Cloudflare Tunnel for Morgan Agent
description: Establish a Cloudflare Tunnel for initial connectivity for the 'morgan' service, pointing to the Kubernetes API server for initial connectivity verification.
details: Create a Cloudflare Tunnel, configure its ingress rules to point to the Kubernetes API server (e.g., `https://kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local`). Ensure the `cloudflared` client is running and connected. Obtain the public hostname for the tunnel. No placeholder service is to be deployed.
test_strategy: Verify the tunnel is active and reachable from the internet using `cloudflared tunnel status` and by attempting to access the configured public hostname, confirming it connects to the Kubernetes API server (e.g., by receiving a 403 Forbidden response for unauthenticated access).

title: Deploy 'sigma1-postgres' Cluster
description: Deploy a single-instance PostgreSQL cluster named 'sigma1-postgres' in the 'databases' namespace, configured with persistent storage (50Gi) and an initial 'sigma1' database and 'sigma1_user'.
details: Create a `Cluster` Custom Resource for CloudNative-PG, specifying a single replica, 50Gi persistent storage, and initial database 'sigma1' with user 'sigma1_user'. Deploy this CR into the 'databases' namespace.
test_strategy: Verify 'sigma1-postgres' pods are running and healthy in the 'databases' namespace. Connect to the PostgreSQL instance using `psql` from within the cluster to confirm 'sigma1' database and 'sigma1_user' exist and are accessible.

title: Deploy 'sigma1-valkey' Cluster
description: Deploy a single-instance Redis/Valkey cluster named 'sigma1-valkey' in the 'databases' namespace using the `valkey/valkey:7.2-alpine` image.
details: Create a `Redis` (or equivalent) Custom Resource for the Redis operator, specifying a single replica and the `valkey/valkey:7.2-alpine` image. Deploy this CR into the 'databases' namespace.
test_strategy: Verify 'sigma1-valkey' pods are running and healthy in the 'databases' namespace. Connect to the Valkey instance using `redis-cli` from within the cluster to confirm accessibility and basic functionality.

title: Create 'sigma1-s3-credentials' Kubernetes Secret
description: Create a Kubernetes Secret named 'sigma1-s3-credentials' in the 'sigma1' namespace, containing the sensitive S3/R2 access key ID and secret access key.
details: Use `kubectl create secret generic sigma1-s3-credentials -n sigma1 --from-literal=S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<key> --from-literal=S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<secret>` using the credentials generated in Task 2. Ensure the secret is created in the 'sigma1' namespace.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1-s3-credentials' Secret exists in the 'sigma1' namespace using `kubectl get secret sigma1-s3-credentials -n sigma1`. Inspect its content to ensure the correct base64-encoded credentials are present using `kubectl get secret sigma1-s3-credentials -n sigma1 -o jsonpath='{.data}'`.

title: Create 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap
description: Create a ConfigMap named 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' in the 'sigma1' namespace, containing connection strings for PostgreSQL, Redis, S3 bucket name, S3 credentials, and Cloudflare Tunnel hostname.
details: Construct a Kubernetes ConfigMap named 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' in the 'sigma1' namespace. Populate it with `POSTGRES_URL` (e.g., `postgres://sigma1_user@sigma1-postgres.databases.svc.cluster.local:5432/sigma1`), `REDIS_URL` (e.g., `redis://sigma1-valkey.databases.svc.cluster.local:6379`), `S3_BUCKET_NAME` (from Task 2), `S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID` (from Task 8), `S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` (from Task 8), and `CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_HOSTNAME` (from Task 5). Ensure URLs and credentials are correctly formatted for service consumption.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap exists in the 'sigma1' namespace using `kubectl get cm sigma1-infra-endpoints -n sigma1 -o yaml`. Inspect its content to ensure all required keys are present and values are correctly formatted. From a test pod in the `sigma1` namespace, attempt to `ping` or `curl` the PostgreSQL, Redis, and Cloudflare Tunnel endpoints using the ConfigMap values to confirm network accessibility. Verify S3 credentials allow access to the bucket.

title: Review Core Infrastructure Setup
description: Review all deployed core infrastructure components, configurations, and connectivity to ensure they meet requirements and best practices.
details: Perform a comprehensive review of all Kubernetes namespaces, operator deployments, PostgreSQL and Redis clusters, S3/R2 bucket, Cloudflare Tunnel, and the 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap. Verify adherence to security policies, naming conventions, and operational readiness. Check logs for errors and resource utilization.
test_strategy: Confirm all previous subtask test strategies have passed. Conduct an end-to-end connectivity test from a sample application pod in the 'sigma1' namespace to PostgreSQL, Redis, S3/R2, and the Cloudflare Tunnel endpoint using the 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap. Verify all components are accessible and functional.
